fldTKmWermFfdERjC.fieldName: How did the athlete complete this reflection quiz?

fldYF0DExP9KotsoZ.fieldName: Q1 — Shot Tracker Usage: A player wants to improve, but sometimes they are tempted to only count makes or leave out bad shooting days. What is the best reason to track shots honestly?

fldwABmogrWtC9zGM.fieldName: Q2 — Website Exploration: Why should the athlete regularly use the challenge site to check their progress and work?

fldshS8Fq9EHM0GG4.fieldName: Q3 — The Choice is Yours: If an athlete is having an off day in practice, what is the best mindset to use?

fldl4YXFMKiKtDUfb.fieldName: Q4 — Shooting Form and Technique: Why should players learn to focus on their form and technique during practice, even when they feel rushed or pressured?

fldIdHdBJRJdZIiqO.fieldName: Q5 — Self Esteem and Accomplishment: What helps a player actually build self-esteem and confidence during the challenge?

fldwGCnnpHkmiwC5n.fieldName: Q6 — Layup Series Homework: Why does the Layup Homework matter for every participant, even if they already make a lot of layups?

fldKNrTgpchnsWefJ.fieldName: Q7 — Touch and Talk: If a teammate looks discouraged or frustrated, what is usually the best response?

fldtzdrBXUP6i3kSD.fieldName: Q8 — 5 Spot Shooting Locations: Why do players practice shooting from all 5 main spots, and not just their favorite one?

fldSCyUencG1gJwDq.fieldName: Q9 — Mikan Drill: What is the best intention for doing the Mikan Drill during the challenge?

fldCkJN13P44pUVYO.fieldName: Q10 — Goal Setting — What GOATS Do!: How do the best athletes approach goal setting in a program like this?

fldEjHXFqVmNOrkwT.fieldName: Q11 — Thank You Note: What is the biggest lesson behind the Thank You Note activity in this program?

fld77T2e4qJkBrGI5.fieldName: Q12 — Coach Yourself: When a player is struggling or frustrated, what is a strong approach to coaching themselves?

fldebZGfUzSgktSd0.fieldName: Q13 — Visualization: Why use visualization in the challenge or before a game or practice?

fld0iZ3xKERog9yf8.fieldName: Q14 — Build Your Freethrow Routine: What is the purpose of building a repeatable free throw routine?

fldUnzXdULPDJteoa.fieldName: Q15 — Bad Habit I Need to Fix: What is the best way to approach a bad habit noticed during the challenge?

fldCy4Ow15WOwUgHD.fieldName: Q16 — Sportsmanship: What does true sportsmanship look like in the context of this challenge?

fldSbzcVFDOe24j3w.fieldName: Q17 — Final Reflection Challenge: What is the purpose of the final reflection challenge for athletes?

fldTcuPdeOKT1dLZ6.fieldName: Q18 — Shot Tracker Summary: What is the real value of completing the full Shot Tracker and reviewing its data at the end?

Final Reflection Quiz Submissions.fieldName: How did the athlete complete this reflection quiz?
Q1 — Shot Tracker Usage: A player wants to improve, but sometimes they are tempted to only count makes or leave out bad shooting days. What is the best reason to track shots honestly?
Q2 — Website Exploration: Why should the athlete regularly use the challenge site to check their progress and work?
Q3 — The Choice is Yours: If an athlete is having an off day in practice, what is the best mindset to use?
Q4 — Shooting Form and Technique: Why should players learn to focus on their form and technique during practice, even when they feel rushed or pressured?
Q5 — Self Esteem and Accomplishment: What helps a player actually build self-esteem and confidence during the challenge?
Q6 — Layup Series Homework: Why does the Layup Homework matter for every participant, even if they already make a lot of layups?
Q7 — Touch and Talk: If a teammate looks discouraged or frustrated, what is usually the best response?
Q8 — 5 Spot Shooting Locations: Why do players practice shooting from all 5 main spots, and not just their favorite one?
Q9 — Mikan Drill: What is the best intention for doing the Mikan Drill during the challenge?
Q10 — Goal Setting — What GOATS Do!: How do the best athletes approach goal setting in a program like this?
Q11 — Thank You Note: What is the biggest lesson behind the Thank You Note activity in this program?
Q12 — Coach Yourself: When a player is struggling or frustrated, what is a strong approach to coaching themselves?
Q13 — Visualization: Why use visualization in the challenge or before a game or practice?
Q14 — Build Your Freethrow Routine: What is the purpose of building a repeatable free throw routine?
Q15 — Bad Habit I Need to Fix: What is the best way to approach a bad habit noticed during the challenge?
Q16 — Sportsmanship: What does true sportsmanship look like in the context of this challenge?
Q17 — Final Reflection Challenge: What is the purpose of the final reflection challenge for athletes?
Q18 — Shot Tracker Summary: What is the real value of completing the full Shot Tracker and reviewing its data at the end?
